text:
As the resolution of modern optical sensors reaches ultra-high-definition (UHD) standards such as 4K and 8K, existing low-light image enhancement (LLIE) methods face significant challenges in balancing computational efficiency with reconstruction quality. Most current LLIE research relies on low-resolution datasets, leaving a gap in benchmarks for UHD applications. To address this, we introduce UHD-LOL, the first large-scale dataset specifically designed for UHD low-light enhancement, featuring 4K and 8K image pairs captured in diverse indoor and outdoor environments. 

In addition to this benchmark, we propose LLFormer, an efficient transformer-based architecture tailored for high-resolution image restoration. LLFormer utilizes a hierarchical design centered on two novel modules: the Axis-based Multi-head Self-Attention (A-MSA) and the Cross-layer Attention Fusion Block (CAFB). By performing self-attention along height and width axes sequentially, A-MSA achieves linear complexity relative to image size, while CAFB adaptively aggregates features across different layers to enhance representation. Quantitative and qualitative evaluations demonstrate that LLFormer achieves state-of-the-art performance on UHD-LOL and established public datasets like LOL and MIT-Adobe FiveK. Furthermore, we demonstrate that incorporating LLFormer as a preprocessing step significantly boosts the accuracy of downstream tasks, such as face detection, in extremely low-light conditions.
